title: Supabase IPv6 connection timeout
symptom: When executing the Postgres node, it spins indefinitely or returns an 'ETIMEDOUT' error. This indicates a failure to connect to the Supabase database.
applies_when: Using Supabase as the Postgres database
root_cause: The timeout occurs because the Direct URL provided by Supabase is IPv6 only, and many Docker containers do not have IPv6 enabled by default, preventing a successful connection.
fix_steps: Go to Supabase and navigate to the Connect section.
Change the connection mode to Session.
Copy the Host URL that looks like 'aws-0-[region].pooler.supabase.com'.
Set the Port to 5432 and use this in your n8n Postgres credentials.
agent_instruction: Use the Supavisor Pooler URL in Session Mode for your Postgres connection.
